key_points.raw:
ABOUT [ edit ]

Bank of India is an India-based bank. The Bank's segments include Treasury Operations, Wholesale Banking and Retail Banking. The Treasury operations segment includes the entire investment portfolio, which is dealing in government and other securities, money market operations and foreign exchange operations.(Source : Company Web-site )

KEY POINTS [ edit ]

Market Position[1]
The bank ranks as the sixth-largest nationalized bank in India, with advances of Rs 6.96 lakh Cr and a total business (Advances + Deposits) of Rs 15.49 lakh Cr as of Q3 FY25.

Revenue Mix Q3 FY26[2]
Retail Banking: 39%
Wholesale banking: 34%
Treasury: 26%
Others: 1%

Key Ratios
Capital Adequacy Ratio: 17.1% in Q3 FY26 vs 16.5% in FY22 [3][4]
NIM: 2.57% in Q3 FY26 vs 2.5% in FY22 [5][6]
Gross NPA: 2.26% in Q3 FY26 vs 9.98% in FY22
Net NPA: 0.60% in Q3 FY26 vs 2.34% in FY22
PCR: 93.6% in Q3 FY26 vs 87.76% in FY22 [7][8]

Branch Network[9]
As of Q3 FY26, the bank has a network of 37,928 customer touchpoints including 5,447 branches, 7,695 ATMs, and 24,762 business correspondents.
Rural: 35%
Semi-Urban: 30%
Metro: 19%
Urban: 16%

Loan Book
In Q3 FY26, the bank's total advances stood at Rs. 7,40,314 Cr vs Rs. ~4,57,000 Cr in FY22. [10][11] Goss domestic loan book stood at Rs 6,29,080 Cr.
Retail: 25%
Agriculture: 18%
MSME: 16%
Corporate & Others: 41%[12]

Deposits
As of Q3 FY26, total deposits stood at Rs. ~8,87,288 Cr vs Rs. ~6,27,800 Cr in FY22. [13] [11] Total domestic deposits were ~Rs 7,65,499 Cr
CASA: 38%
Retail Term Deposit: 46%
Bulk Term Deposit: 16%[14]

International Business
The Bank has 21 overseas branches, 1 Representative Office, 4 Overseas Subsidiaries 1 JV, and 1 IFSC Banking Unit. Its international operations are spread across 5 continents and 15 countries viz., USA, Cayman Islands, U.K., France, Belgium, Tanzania, Kenya, etc. As of Q3 FY26, foreign business contributes ~17% to the total business mix of the bank. [15][16]

New Products
In FY24, the bank launched new products “BOI Star Family Savings Account”, and “BOI Star Rooftop Solar Panel Finance” with Loans up to Rs.10 lakhs for individuals and Rs.1 Cr for registered housing societies, etc. [17]

Digital Initiatives
Bank’s BOI Mobile Omni Neo App is equipped with 300+ features, for all banking, investment, and payment needs. In FY24, it upgraded its digital infrastructure by adding a new data center and upgrading intrabank networks. The bank has 97 lakh Internet banking Users and 121 lakh Mobile banking Users as of Q3 FY26. [18][19]

Investment in Technology
The bank has budgeted Rs. 2000 Cr for technology transformation, including Rs.1,200 Cr for Opex and Rs. 800 Cr for Capex. 75% of this budget has been spent as of FY24. It is investing in new technologies like AI/ML/Generative AI to ensure a faster and more robust digital platform.[20] [19]

GoI Shareholding
In FY22, the bank raised equity capital of Rs. 2,550 Cr through the QIP (non-government) route, resulting in a reduction in GoI’s shareholding in the bank from 89.10% as of FY21 to 81.41% as of FY22. Post another equity capital infusion through a QIP of Rs. 4,500 Cr in Q3FY24, GoI’s shareholding came down to 73.38% as of Jun 2024. [21] [22][23]

Outlook[24][25][26]
Management has guided for global advances growth of approximately 13-14% and global deposit growth of 11-12% for FY26. To sustain this momentum, the bank is prioritizing the mobilisation of low-cost deposits and accelerating the growth of high-yielding advances, particularly within the RAM (Retail, Agriculture, and MSME) segment, which is targeted to reach 65% of the total credit portfolio by 2031. This growth is further supported by a robust credit pipeline of nearly Rs. 80,000 crore, including a Rs. 65,000 crore corporate pipeline expected to be disbursed in late FY26 and early FY27. Additionally, the bank plans to continue its physical expansion by opening 200 new branches annually through FY27, while aiming to maintain a Net Interest Margin (NIM) of around 2.50% for the full year.
